content.problemStatement:
You are given NNN intervals [L1,R1],[L2,R2],‚Ä¶,[LN,RN][L_1, R_1], [L_2, R_2], \ldots, [L_N, R_N][L1‚Äč,R1‚Äč],[L2‚Äč,R2‚Äč],‚Ä¶,[LN‚Äč,RN‚Äč]. Consider NNN integers x1,x2,‚Ä¶,xNx_1, x_2, \ldots, x_Nx1‚Äč,x2‚Äč,‚Ä¶,xN‚Äč such that:


xi‚ąą[Li,Ri]x_i \in [L_i, R_i]xi‚Äč‚ąą[Li‚Äč,Ri‚Äč] for each valid iii
all decimal digits of S=x1+x2+x3+‚Ä¶+xNS = x_1 + x_2 + x_3 + \ldots + x_NS=x1‚Äč+x2‚Äč+x3‚Äč+‚Ä¶+xN‚Äč (without leading zeroes) lie between AAA and BBB inclusive
In how many different ways can you pick the sequence x1,x2,‚Ä¶,xNx_1, x_2, \ldots, x_Nx1‚Äč,x2‚Äč,‚Ä¶,xN‚Äč? Since this number may be very large, compute it modulo 109+710^9+7109+7.
constraints: 0‚Č§Li‚Č§Ri<1090 \le L_i \le R_i \lt 10^90‚Č§Li‚Äč‚Č§Ri‚Äč<109 for each valid iii
content.inputFormat:
The first line of the input contains a single integer TTT denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
The first line of each test case contains three space-separated integers NNN, AAA and BBB.
NNN lines follow. For each iii (1‚Č§i‚Č§N1 \le i \le N1‚Č§i‚Č§N), the iii-th of these lines contains two space-separated integers LiL_iLi‚Äč and RiR_iRi‚Äč.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print a single line contains one integer ‚ÄĒ the number of ways to choose x1,x2,‚Ä¶,xNx_1, x_2, \ldots, x_Nx1‚Äč,x2‚Äč,‚Ä¶,xN‚Äč, modulo 109+710^9+7109+7.
